quiz.title: Wake Up! / Neha's Alarm Clock - Unit 1 Quiz

questions.explanation: The poem 'Wake Up!' is about waking up in the morning and noticing all the beautiful things around us.
hints: Look at the title of the poem

questions.questionText: In the story, what did Neha use to wake up on time?
questions.explanation: Neha used an alarm clock to wake up on time for school.
hints: The title of the story gives you a clue

options: A group of words that makes complete sense
questions.explanation: A sentence is a group of words that makes complete sense and expresses a complete thought.

questions.questionText: Which of these is a complete sentence?
questions.explanation: 'She went to school' is a complete sentence because it has a subject (She) and tells us what she did (went to school).
hints: A sentence needs a subject and tells what happens

questions.questionText: True or False: Every sentence must start with a capital letter.
questions.explanation: True! Every sentence starts with a capital letter and ends with a full stop, question mark, or exclamation mark.
hints: Look at how sentences are written in your book

questions.questionText: In the poem, the sky is described as:
questions.explanation: The poem describes the morning sky as bright, with the sun shining - a beautiful start to the day.
hints: Think about what the sky looks like in the morning

questions.questionText: A sentence that asks a question ends with a:
questions.explanation: Questions end with a question mark (?). For example: 'What is your name?'

questions.questionText: Why is it important to wake up on time?
options: To be ready for school and activities
questions.explanation: Waking up on time helps us get ready for school, finish our morning tasks, and not be late.
hints: Think about what happens when you wake up late

questions.explanation: 'What a beautiful day!' shows excitement and ends with an exclamation mark (!)

questions.questionText: True or False: In the poem, nature wakes up along with us in the morning.
questions.explanation: True! The poem shows that nature - birds, flowers, the sun - all wake up in the morning along with us.
hints: Think about what happens in nature in the morning
